Definition: The action-power of intentional daily opposition of a habit — the intentional wedge that purposely sheds bad habits through deliberate denial/replacement, strategical countering or sabotage; its effectiveness tied to the law of momentum/inertia.
A specific daily action designed to oppose and disrupt a target habit
Self-rated daily score (1-10) of one's resistance strength and commitment
A day with 3 or more urges — surviving clean earns double points
Consecutive days of completed morning declarations and logged resistance
